request: We need an app for staff to check attendees in at the venue entrance on the event day.

problem.value: Verify and admit legitimate attendees quickly at the venue entrance while preventing unauthorized/duplicate entry.
problem.evidence: "check attendees in at the venue entrance on the event day" implies speed + control, not stated explicitly.

current_process.evidence: No mention of today's process (paper list, spreadsheet, another ticketing tool).

success_metrics.value: Likely: fast average check-in time, zero double-entries, accurate real-time headcount.
success_metrics.evidence: Implicit from 'event day' urgency, not stated.

actors.value: Staff (check-in operators) at entrance; attendees being checked in. Possible organizer/admin role not mentioned.

business_objects.value: Attendee/Registration, Ticket, Event, Check-in record — exact fields and source unknown.
business_objects.evidence: Implied by domain (event entrance check-in), not detailed by client.

business_rules.evidence: No info on ticket types, re-entry policy, one-ticket-one-entry, guest/plus-one handling.

workflow.value: Presumed: scan/lookup -> validate -> mark checked-in. Online vs offline mode unknown.
workflow.evidence: Not specified; venue connectivity is a classic blind spot.

integrations.evidence: Unclear where the attendee/ticket list comes from (registration platform, spreadsheet import, ticketing API) and whether it syncs live.

permissions.evidence: No mention of staff roles, override rights, or admin oversight/reporting access.

config_vs_custom.value: Not indicated as a multi-client configurable platform; likely single-event or single-org build.
config_vs_custom.evidence: No context suggesting reuse across clients/events with differing rules.

constraints.value: Venue connectivity (Wi-Fi/cellular) likely unreliable; hardware for scanning (phone/tablet/scanner) needed; hard deadline = event date, no second chance.
constraints.evidence: Venue entrance context implies possible offline conditions; not stated but a known trap.

edge_cases.value: Duplicate check-in attempts, lost/forgotten ticket, walk-ins without pre-registration, revoked/cancelled tickets, connectivity loss mid-event.
edge_cases.evidence: Standard for entrance check-in apps, not mentioned by client.

reporting.value: Likely need: live attendance count, exportable check-in log, per-staff/device activity.
reporting.evidence: No compliance/audit obligation named, so kept at medium rather than high.

risks.value: Risk of network downtime at venue causing check-in failure; duplicate/fraudulent entries; staff untrained before go-live on event day (no second chance to fix).
risks.evidence: Inferred from single-day, high-stakes nature of the event.

questions.q: Where does the attendee/ticket list come from — an existing registration or ticketing system, a spreadsheet import, or will this app be the source of truth — and does it need to sync live or can it be pre-loaded before the event?
questions.why: High impact, empty: determines the whole architecture (API integration vs static import) and whether real-time sync/idempotency logic is needed.

questions.q: Will the entrance reliably have internet/Wi-Fi, or must staff be able to check people in offline and sync later?
questions.why: High impact, empty: offline-capable check-in vs online-only is a fundamentally different build (local cache, conflict resolution, pending state) — the biggest blind spot for a venue-entrance app.

questions.q: What are the entry rules — is each ticket single-use only, is re-entry allowed, are there different ticket/attendee categories, and what should staff do when they scan a duplicate or already-checked-in attendee?
questions.why: High impact, empty: defines validation logic and staff-facing error handling; this varies a lot by event type.

questions.q: Do all staff have the same check-in rights, or are there roles (e.g., regular staff vs a supervisor who can override a rejected check-in or see live headcounts)?
questions.why: High impact, empty: permission gaps are typically discovered late in testing and are costly to retrofit before a fixed event date.

questions.q: How is check-in handled today (paper list, spreadsheet, another tool), and what would make this rollout a clear success on the day (speed per attendee, zero duplicates, live headcount)?
questions.why: High impact, empty: shapes both the migration path and the concrete acceptance bar for the deliverable.

summary.objective: Give venue staff a fast, reliable way to check attendees in at the entrance on the event day, preventing duplicate or unauthorized entry.
summary.scope: Likely a lightweight mobile/tablet app for staff: look up or scan an attendee's ticket/QR code, validate against the guest list, mark them as checked-in, and give a simple pass/fail signal. Probably includes a live headcount view and an exportable check-in log for the organizer.
assumptions: The attendee/ticket list will be imported or synced from an existing registration/ticketing source rather than fully authored in this app.
Check-in follows a simple scan/lookup -> validate -> mark-checked-in flow.
Venue connectivity may be unreliable, so offline capability may be required.
This is a single-event or single-organization build rather than a multi-client configurable platform.
Basic reporting (live count, exportable log) is expected; no regulatory audit obligation has been named.
summary.blind_spot: Nobody has said whether the entrance will have dependable internet access. If it doesn't, the app needs offline-first scanning with local validation and later sync — a fundamentally different (and costlier) build than a simple online lookup, and this can't be retrofitted the morning of the event.

problem.value: Enable staff to quickly and reliably admit attendees at the venue entrance on event day, avoiding queues and letting in only valid ticket holders.
problem.evidence: Request: 'staff to check attendees in at the venue entrance on the event day'.

current_process.evidence: Not stated — likely manual list/paper or spreadsheet today, or nothing at all.

success_metrics.evidence: Not stated — likely check-in speed, no duplicate/fraudulent entries, real-time attendance count.

actors.value: Staff (door/check-in agents) explicitly named; likely also an organizer/admin role for oversight and attendees as the checked-in subject.
actors.evidence: 'staff to check attendees in' explicit; admin/organizer role inferred.

business_objects.value: Likely: Attendee, Ticket/Registration, Event, Guest list, Check-in record.
business_objects.evidence: Implied by domain (event entry) though not stated.

business_rules.evidence: Not stated — how is validity checked (QR/barcode/ID/name lookup), is one entry per ticket enforced, are re-entries or plus-ones allowed?

workflow.value: Likely: staff scans/looks up attendee → system validates against guest list/ticket → marks as checked-in → blocks re-scan or flags duplicate.
workflow.evidence: Standard event check-in pattern inferred from request.

integrations.evidence: Not stated — unclear if attendee/ticket data comes from an existing registration/ticketing system, and whether connectivity at the venue is reliable (offline mode needed).

permissions.value: Likely all door staff have equal check-in rights; an admin/organizer may need a dashboard view.
permissions.evidence: Only 'staff' mentioned, no role distinction given.

config_vs_custom.evidence: No configurable multi-client platform context established for this request.

constraints.value: Time-critical, single event day, likely needs mobile/handheld devices and may face poor venue connectivity.
constraints.evidence: 'venue entrance on the event day' implies live, time-boxed, on-site constraints.

edge_cases.evidence: Not addressed — lost tickets, plus-ones, VIP fast lane, duplicate/simultaneous scans at multiple entrances, walk-ins not pre-registered.

reporting.evidence: No compliance/audit driver named yet; likely just a live attendance count, not a regulatory requirement.

acceptance.evidence: Not defined yet — e.g. staff can check in an attendee in under X seconds, no duplicate admits, works with N simultaneous devices.

risks.evidence: Not stated — device failure, connectivity loss, entrance bottleneck, incomplete/late guest list sync.

questions.q: How will staff validate an attendee at the door — scanning a QR/barcode ticket, searching a name on a guest list, or checking ID — and what should happen if the same ticket is scanned twice?
questions.why: uncertainty(empty) × impact(high): this defines the core check-in mechanic and fraud/duplicate handling — it changes the whole build.

questions.q: Is there an existing registration/ticketing system the app should pull attendee data from, and does the venue have reliable internet on event day, or does the app need to work offline and sync later?
questions.why: uncertainty(empty) × impact(high): determines whether this is a simple online lookup or a offline-first sync build with conflict handling — a very different architecture.

questions.q: Will the check-in flow differ by entrance, staff role, or attendee type (e.g. VIP fast lane, staff-only admin dashboard vs door agents), or is every staff member equal?
questions.why: uncertainty(inferred) × impact(medium-high): role/entrance differentiation changes screens, routing and access control needs.

questions.q: How will you know the check-in app worked well — speed per attendee, zero duplicate/fraudulent entries, real-time headcount for organizers, something else?
questions.why: uncertainty(empty) × impact(medium): shapes acceptance criteria and what reporting/dashboard is actually needed.

questions.q: What should happen for edge cases — attendee not on the list, lost/forgotten ticket, plus-ones, or someone trying to re-enter after leaving?
questions.why: uncertainty(empty) × impact(medium): these are the scenarios most likely to cause on-the-day failure at a live, one-shot event.

summary.objective: Give venue staff a fast, reliable way to check attendees in at the entrance on event day, admitting only valid ticket/registration holders.
summary.scope: Likely a lightweight mobile/handheld app for staff to scan or look up an attendee, validate against a guest list or ticket source, mark them as checked-in, and prevent duplicate entries — possibly with an organizer-facing live count.
assumptions: There is likely an existing registration/ticketing source of attendee data to check against.
The venue may have unreliable connectivity, so offline capability could be needed.
All staff currently have equal check-in permissions; no role differentiation stated.
Success is assumed to mean fast, duplicate-free check-in rather than compliance/audit reporting.
summary.blind_spot: The request sounds like a simple scan-and-admit app, but the real risk is the check-in *event* itself: how attendee validity is sourced and verified, whether the app must work offline at the venue with later sync, and how duplicate/fraudulent or edge-case entries (no ticket, plus-ones, re-entry) are handled — these decisions change the entire architecture, not just the UI.

problem.value: Speed up and secure attendee entry, avoiding duplicate or fraudulent check-ins and long queues at the venue door.
problem.evidence: "staff to check attendees in at the venue entrance on the event day" implies a verification/gatekeeping need, not just a form.

current_process.evidence: No mention of how check-in is done today (paper list, spreadsheet, another ticketing tool).

success_metrics.evidence: No stated KPI (check-in speed, zero duplicate entries, queue length).

actors.value: Staff (checker) explicitly named; attendee implied as the checked-in subject; likely also a supervisor/organizer role for oversight.

business_objects.evidence: Implicit from "check attendees in" — requires a ticket/registration entity to check against.

business_rules.evidence: Unknown: single-use tickets, multiple ticket types, VIP/fast-lane, re-entry allowed, plus-ones, walk-ins.

workflow.value: Attendee arrives → staff scans QR/ticket → system validates against list → marks as checked-in → grants/denies entry.
workflow.evidence: Standard check-in flow inferred from the request; exception handling (duplicate, invalid) not specified.

integrations.evidence: Not stated whether attendee data comes from an existing registration/ticketing platform or is entered manually; this determines sync design.

permissions.evidence: No detail on whether all staff share one generic access level or need per-entrance/role distinctions and supervisor overrides.

config_vs_custom.value: Likely a single-event, single-venue build rather than a multi-tenant configurable platform.
config_vs_custom.evidence: No mention of multiple clients/venues; treated as one-off unless stated otherwise.

constraints.evidence: Venue-day connectivity (wifi/cellular reliability) is unstated but critical — determines online vs offline-first architecture; also unstated deadline/device constraints.

edge_cases.evidence: Duplicate scans, invalid/lost tickets, walk-in registrations, network outage, capacity limits not addressed.

reporting.evidence: Real-time attendance count and export likely wanted by organizers, but not stated; no explicit compliance driver yet.

risks.evidence: Network failure, device battery/availability, staff training, entry-rush load spikes not addressed.

questions.q: Where does the attendee/ticket list come from — an existing registration or ticketing system to sync from, or will data be entered directly in this app?
questions.why: High impact, fully uncertain: determines whether this is a standalone data-entry tool or a sync/import build, and how conflicts (duplicate names, last-minute registrations) are resolved.

questions.q: What makes a check-in valid or invalid — single-use tickets, multiple ticket types (VIP/staff/press), allowed re-entry, or walk-ins without a ticket?
questions.why: High impact, empty: the validation rule set defines the whole scanning logic and exception handling; without it the workflow can't be built correctly.

questions.q: Will the venue entrance have reliable wifi/cellular connectivity, or must the app work offline and sync check-ins later?
questions.why: High impact, empty: this decides between a simple online app and an offline-first architecture with conflict resolution on sync — a major cost driver.

questions.q: Do different staff or entrances need different access (e.g., only certain staff can check in VIPs, or a supervisor can undo a check-in)?
questions.why: High impact, empty: without this, everyone gets full access by default, which may be wrong for VIP/press control or error correction.

questions.q: Does the organizer need a live dashboard of who has checked in, or an export/audit trail after the event?
questions.why: Medium impact but shapes deliverables: distinguishes a bare scanning tool from one with real-time visibility and post-event reporting.

summary.objective: Enable event staff to verify and check attendees in quickly and reliably at the venue entrance on event day.
summary.scope: A staff-facing check-in app: scan or look up an attendee/ticket, validate against the registration list, mark as checked-in, and prevent duplicate/invalid entries. Likely includes a simple staff interface and possibly a live attendance view for organizers.
assumptions: This is a single-event, single-venue build rather than a multi-tenant configurable platform.
Standard check-in flow: scan/lookup → validate → mark checked-in → grant or deny entry.
Attendee and ticket data exist somewhere (registration/ticketing source) prior to event day.
summary.blind_spot: Connectivity at the venue entrance is unaddressed — if wifi/cellular is unreliable, the app needs offline-first scanning with later sync and conflict resolution (e.g., same ticket scanned twice on different devices), which is a fundamentally different build than a simple online lookup tool.
